# magus

> Workspace-aware build orchestrator and content-addressed cache. Every link below is the page's raw Markdown (its index.md sibling), so an agent fetches clean content directly.

## Documentation

- [Affected](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/affected/index.md): How magus runs a target only for the projects a VCS diff touched, taking the transitive closure over the dependency graph, and the forensic modes that explain, graph, shard, and bisect that set.
- [Agents](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/agents/index.md): How magus equips AI agents - the installable skills that teach agents to query the knowledge graph instead of grepping, run work through targets instead of raw tools, triage generated files, and ground refactors in graph evidence; plus the MCP daemon, MAGUS.md routing, and the drift check that keeps installed skills current.
- [Breaking changes](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/breaking-changes/index.md): How magus makes backward-incompatible changes visible in review before they reach users - buf-breaking for proto schemas and a drift-gated api.lock snapshot for a CLI's public surface.
- [magus stdlib](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/index.md): Reference for every magus stdlib module - fs, os, http, json, yaml, crypto, and the rest of the magusfile API surface.
- [archive module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/archive/index.md): Archive creation and extraction with automatic format detection.
- [charm module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/charm/index.md): Constructors for charm values: RFC 6902 JSON Patches over a target's argv (see docs/charms.md).
- [crypto module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/crypto/index.md): Content digests (SHA-256/512; SHA-1 and MD5 for legacy-checksum interop).
- [encoding module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/encoding/index.md): Base64/hex/URL text codecs.
- [env module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/env/index.md): Process environment variable access.
- [fmt module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/fmt/index.md): String formatting (printf-style).
- [fs module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/fs/index.md): Filesystem and path primitives.
- [http module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/http/index.md): HTTP client with automatic retry on transient errors.
- [json module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/json/index.md): JSON encode/decode.
- [magus module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/magus/index.md): Magus core primitives.
- [markdown module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/markdown/index.md): GitHub-Flavored Markdown to semantic HTML.
- [os module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/os/index.md): Process execution.
- [path module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/path/index.md): Pure path-string math: abs, rel, clean, is_abs, expand_user.
- [platform module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/platform/index.md): Normalize OS/architecture identifiers across naming conventions (aarch64↔arm64, Darwin↔darwin).
- [semver module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/semver/index.md): Semantic version parsing and comparison (SemVer 2.0.0).
- [strings module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/strings/index.md): Case conversion and word helpers (camel/snake/kebab/Pascal, capitalize, words, ellipsis).
- [template module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/template/index.md): Logic-less Mustache templating (Mustache spec, via github.com/cbroglie/mustache).
- [time module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/time/index.md): Timestamp formatting/parsing and duration parsing (Go time, UTC).
- [toml module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/toml/index.md): TOML parse and stringify (TOML 1.0 via pelletier/go-toml/v2).
- [uuid module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/uuid/index.md): Unique identifiers and random tokens (v4 random, v7 time-ordered, plus raw random hex/tokens).
- [vcs module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/vcs/index.md): Version-control queries for the current working tree.
- [yaml module](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/buzz/modules/yaml/index.md): YAML parse and stringify (YAML 1.2 via gopkg.in/yaml.v3).
- [Cache model](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/cache/index.md): How magus computes a content-addressed cache key from a target's declared inputs, replays outputs on a hit without rerunning the body, and stores it all as plain files under .magus.
- [Charms](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/charms/index.md): Learn how magus charms replace one-off tool flags with shared, composable intent modifiers that patch a target's argument vector at run time.
- [charm diagnostics](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/charms/index.md): Landing page for MGS6xxx diagnostics that flag problems with how a charm's JSON Patch interacts with a target's command.
- [MGS6001: charm patch does not apply to the command](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/charms/MGS6001/index.md): Fires when magus describe target previews a charm whose JSON Patch is valid in shape but cannot apply to the target's argv, so the charm is dead on that target.
- [knowledge-graph diagnostics](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/knowledge/index.md): Landing page for MGS7xxx diagnostics that flag ambiguities the knowledge-graph extractor hits while building the deterministic graph, such as a buzz import that resolves to no file or a doc that cites an unregistered diagnostic code.
- [MGS7001: unresolvable buzz import](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/knowledge/MGS7001/index.md): Fires when the knowledge-graph extractor cannot resolve a workspace-relative buzz import to a scanned file, so the import becomes an inferred edge to the literal path instead of a resolved file-to-file edge.
- [MGS7002: dangling doc reference](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/knowledge/MGS7002/index.md): Fires when a documentation page mentions an MGS#### diagnostic code that is not registered, so the knowledge-graph extractor cannot link the mention to a real diagnostic node.
- [magusfile diagnostics](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/magusfile/index.md): Landing page for MGS1xxx diagnostics that flag authoring mistakes in a workspace's magusfile, such as missing targets or unresolved declarations.
- [MGS1001: no ci target defined](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/magusfile/MGS1001/index.md): Fires when magus ci runs but no project in the selected scope declares a ci target, meaning the CI anchor would silently do nothing.
- [MGS1002: spell import shadowed](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/magusfile/MGS1002/index.md): Fires when a spells/<name> in a nested project is shadowed by a same-named spell higher in the tree, because spell imports resolve root-wins so the deeper definition is dead.
- [MGS1003: bespoke phase-fragment target name](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/magusfile/MGS1003/index.md): Fires when a magusfile declares a target named after a static-analysis or formatting subset (typecheck, vet, audit, security, style, prettify) instead of composing it into lint or format.
- [MGS1004: unreached footprint declaration](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/magusfile/MGS1004/index.md): Fires when a magus.inputs or magus.outputs call is not statically reachable from a target body, so the declared glob never enters a cache key.
- [MGS1005: redundant footprint glob](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/magusfile/MGS1005/index.md): Fires when a per-target magus.inputs or magus.outputs glob is already declared project-wide, making it a no-op under the additive footprint model.
- [output-reference diagnostics](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/outputref/index.md): Landing page for MGS8xxx diagnostics that flag problems resolving a target-output reference id with magus query, such as a ref that aged out of the cache, an ambiguous prefix, or a malformed ref passed to a ref-only flag.
- [MGS8001: output ref not found](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/outputref/MGS8001/index.md): Fires when magus query output <ref> names a well-formed reference id but no captured output is stored for it, because it aged out of the cache or the ref is mistyped.
- [MGS8002: ambiguous output ref prefix](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/outputref/MGS8002/index.md): Fires when a shortened magus query output <ref> prefix matches more than one stored output reference, so magus cannot tell which one you meant.
- [MGS8003: malformed output ref](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/outputref/MGS8003/index.md): Fires when magus query output is given an argument that is not a valid ref<hex> id, so the value cannot name a stored output.
- [race diagnostics](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/race/index.md): Landing page for MGS4xxx race condition diagnostics emitted by the magus race detector across static, watch, and replay modes.
- [MGS4001: filesystem race condition](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/race/MGS4001/index.md): Fires when two or more concurrent projects modify the same git-tracked file during overlapping execution windows under the same target.
- [MGS4002: declared output overlap](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/race/MGS4002/index.md): Fires at graph construction when two or more projects in the current dispatch declare the same output path glob under the same target.
- [MGS4003: non-deterministic output](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/race/MGS4003/index.md): Fires under race replay when a project's declared outputs differ across two consecutive runs, indicating a reproducibility violation.
- [MGS4004: potential undeclared dependency](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/race/MGS4004/index.md): Fires when a project not in the dispatch consumes a file written by one that ran, suggesting the dependency graph is missing an edge.
- [sandbox diagnostics](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/index.md): Landing page for the sandbox that confines magus spells to the workspace, plus its MGS2xxx diagnostics for denied reads, writes, execs, and env leaks.
- [MGS2001: path read denied](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2001/index.md): Fires when a spell tries to read a file outside the workspace and outside the configured sandbox allowlist, typically a credential store.
- [MGS2002: path write denied](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2002/index.md): Fires when a spell tries to write to a file outside the workspace and outside the sandbox allowlist, blocking persistence and tampering attacks.
- [MGS2003: env vars stripped](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2003/index.md): Informational notice that the sandbox replaced a child process environment with a curated allowlist, dropping variables like tokens or credentials.
- [MGS2004: sandbox.allow unresolved](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2004/index.md): Fires when a sandbox.allow path in magus.yaml references an env var or path that magus cannot resolve, so the rule is skipped rather than fatal.
- [MGS2005: kernel landlock unavailable](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2005/index.md): Notice that the sandbox is enabled but the host lacks kernel landlock, so only interpreter-level enforcement is active on this platform.
- [MGS2006: PATH-shim manager stripped](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2006/index.md): Fires when a subprocess likely failed because the sandbox stripped env vars that a PATH-shim runtime manager such as mise, asdf, or direnv needs.
- [MGS2007: exec denied](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2007/index.md): Fires when a spell tries to execute a binary whose resolved path is outside the workspace, tmp, and the system executable allowlist.
- [MGS2008: daemon socket withheld](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2008/index.md): Fires at debug level when magus re-injects the daemon socket vars into a recursive magus invocation, after withholding them from sandboxed spell subprocesses.
- [MGS2009: outbound network request](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2009/index.md): Fires at info level for every outbound network request a spell makes through the http bindings while the sandbox is active. The sandbox audits egress but does not block it.
- [MGS2010: sandbox policy mismatch](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS2010/index.md): Fires when a long-running daemon is asked to serve a workspace whose sandbox policy was not part of the union it applied at startup, either undeclared or a differing fingerprint.
- [MGS3001: descendant project boundary crossed](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/sandbox/MGS3001/index.md): Fires when a spell's downward walk crosses into a registered descendant project's directory and modifies files there during a write-mode dispatch.
- [services diagnostics](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/services/index.md): Landing page for MGS5xxx diagnostics that flag problems with long-running service ops, such as near-duplicate services that should be shared instead of run as separate processes.
- [MGS5001: near-duplicate services](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/services/MGS5001/index.md): Fires when two or more service ops in a run look like copies of the same service (same image and container port) but differ subtly, so they will run as separate processes instead of one shared instance.
- [MGS5002: service op detaches](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/services/MGS5002/index.md): Fires when a service op's process command detaches (docker run -d), which forks the process away from magus and breaks foreground supervision, stdout capture, readiness probing, and graceful stop.
- [MGS5003: command op never exits](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/codes/services/MGS5003/index.md): Fires when a command op runs a watcher (tsc --watch, vitest --watch), which never terminates, so a run-to-completion op would hang the run. The mirror image of MGS5002.
- [magus.yaml configuration](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/config/index.md): Every magus.yaml config key with its MAGUS_* environment variable, CLI flag, and type. Generated from the config schema.
- [Documentation conventions](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/conventions/index.md): How to read the magus docs - placeholders, shell commands, runnable examples, admonitions, code-block titles, and auto-generated pages.
- [Daemon and concurrency](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/daemon/index.md): The magus daemon holds workspace state and enforces one shared concurrency pool across every client, so parallel CI steps and nested invocations do not oversubscribe the machine.
- [Debugging magusfiles](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/debugging/index.md): Drop into an interactive Buzz REPL with magusfile bindings preloaded, or set magus.pry() breakpoints mid-target to inspect frames, locals, and state.
- [Dependencies](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/dependencies/index.md): The two dependency mechanisms in magus - magus.needs (target-level, imperative) and depends_on (project-level, declarative) - how they interact, and how a cross-project needs folds into both.
- [magus Documentation](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/documentation/index.md): The magus documentation hub covering install, targets, spells, charms, operations, engines, remote caching, MCP, telemetry, and the interactive playground.
- [Download](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/download/index.md): Download a signed release of magus, verify it with the embedded Ed25519 key, and keep it current with magus self update.
- [Run magus in Docker](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/download/docker/index.md): Pull and run the official magus container images from GHCR - a multi-arch static image and a glibc variant - mounting your workspace, plus how to verify the cosign signature.
- [Install on Linux](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/download/linux/index.md): Download, verify, and install the magus binary on Linux (amd64 or arm64) and put it on your PATH - no root, no sudo.
- [Install on macOS](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/download/macos/index.md): Download, verify, and install the magus binary on macOS (Apple Silicon or Intel), clear the quarantine flag, and put it on your PATH.
- [Install on Windows](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/download/windows/index.md): Download, verify, and install the magus binary on Windows (amd64) with PowerShell and put it on your PATH.
- [Editor setup](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/editor/index.md): Wire your editor to the magus language server (magus buzz lsp) for completion, hover, and signature help in magusfiles and spells.
- [Engines](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/engines/index.md): Understand the small engine.Engine seam that lets magus run magusfiles on the embedded Buzz VM and how a new scripting language would plug in.
- [FAQ](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/faq/index.md): Short answers to the questions that come up first with magus - spells versus targets versus charms, why runs are read-only, how the cache decides, affected builds, the daemon, and adding a tool.
- [Coming from Nx](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/from-nx/index.md): A terminology map and porting sketch for a team moving a monorepo workspace from Nx to magus, with an honest list of what each tool has that the other does not.
- [Getting started](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/getting-started/index.md): A linear, CLI-first walkthrough from installing magus to running your first spell-composed target and a ci pipeline.
- [Glossary](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/glossary/index.md): The core magus vocabulary - workspace, project, magusfile, target, spell, operation, charm, ward, module, engine, and the console's own terms - each defined in one line with a pointer to the page that covers it in full.
- [Insight](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/insight/index.md): How magus reads VCS history to show where a codebase's attention and risk concentrate - hotspots, temporal coupling, ownership, and trend - as a behavioral complement to static structure.
- [Knowledge graph](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/knowledge/index.md): The knowledge graph is a deterministic, cache-backed graph of the magus domain - projects, targets, spells, ops, charms, modules, diagnostics, docs, and buzz sources - that agents and humans query instead of grepping. This page covers the schema, the verbs, the file layout, and how to point external graph tools at an export.
- [magus affected](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-affected/index.md): Run a target for every project affected by a VCS diff, with forensic modes for explain, graph, CI shard plan, and regression bisect.
- [magus completion](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-completion/index.md): Print a bash, zsh, fish, or PowerShell completion script to stdout, ready to append to your shell startup file for tab-completion of magus commands.
- [magus config](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-config/index.md): Inspect the effective merged configuration or write keys to the local or global magus.yaml, with subcommands for view, set, init, and cache prune.
- [magus describe](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-describe/index.md): Define a magus concept (spell, charm, target, project, workspace, module, mcp-tool) and list every entity of that kind, or detail one when a name is given.
- [magus doctor](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-doctor/index.md): Run diagnostic checks on the workspace covering project discovery, magusfile syntax, graph cycles, symlinks, env vars, and VCS reachability.
- [magus graph](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-graph/index.md): Emit the project dependency DAG, export the knowledge graph for external graph tools, and report the graph's shape (god nodes, orphans, doc coverage).
- [magus init](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-init/index.md): Bootstrap a magus workspace with a magus.yaml config, magusfile stub, and VCS merge driver; supports global, local, and non-interactive modes.
- [magus insight](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-insight/index.md): Show where a codebase's attention and risk concentrate: hotspots, temporal coupling, ownership, and trend from VCS history, plus volatility from run-outcome history.
- [magus ls](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-ls/index.md): List every discovered project in the workspace with its language pack, source files, outputs, dependencies, and tool requirements.
- [magus run](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-run/index.md): Run a named target (build, test, lint, format, ci, etc.) for the selected projects, defaulting to the cwd project when no arguments are given.
- [magus self](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-self/index.md): Manage the magus binary in place, with a self-update subcommand supporting version pinning, dry-run, downgrade, and out-of-tree install directories.
- [magus server](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-server/index.md): Start, stop, or check liveness of the persistent magus daemon that keeps workspace discovery, config, and cache warm across invocations.
- [magus status](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-status/index.md): Show effective config plus the live concurrency pool state of any running parent magus process, with optional --watch polling and --compact output.
- [magus tail](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-tail/index.md): Stream the captured build log of the most recent cache entry for a project, with -f to follow and target selectors like project:test.
- [magus version](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-version/index.md): Print the magus version string, git commit hash, and build date for the currently installed binary.
- [magus watch](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-watch/index.md): Watch the workspace for file-system changes and emit batches of changed paths to stdout, compatible with git diff and magus affected --stdin.
- [magus where](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-where/index.md): Fuzzy-match a project by leaf-anchored substring and print its absolute path, designed for shell substitution like cd "$(magus where api)".
- [magus x](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus-x/index.md): Interactive shorthand for magus run with a TTY picker for project and target, remembering the last target used per project.
- [magus command](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/manpage/magus/index.md): Standalone build orchestrator and content-addressed cache for polyglot monorepos, with workspace-aware subcommands for build, test, lint, and inspect.
- [MCP](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/mcp/index.md): The magus daemon exposes a Model Context Protocol server over Streamable HTTP so AI agents and IDE plugins can call build tools directly.
- [Operations](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/operations/index.md): Defines an Operation, its place in the Spell to Target to Process hierarchy, and how ops compose into runnable targets.
- [Target output references](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/output-refs/index.md): Every target that runs gets a short reference id (ref1a2b3c) for its captured output. Retrieve any target's exact output later with magus query, pipe it anywhere, or open it in the browser log viewer - no copy-pasting a wall of text.
- [Console API](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/reference/console/index.md): Read-only loopback API that lets the Graph Explorer show your live workspace. Loopback only, bearer token, no mutation.
- [Remote caching](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/remote-cache/index.md): Wire an S3 or GitHub Actions remote cache backend as a spell so CI runners replay signed build artifacts across machines with a strict trust model.
- [Sandbox model](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/sandbox/index.md): The threat model and allowlist semantics that magus enforces around spell execution, and how the MGS2xxx diagnostics map onto that model.
- [Services](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/services/index.md): How magus runs long-running service ops, shares one instance across dependents and invocations, and guards against the sprawl and misuse foot-guns.
- [Spells](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/index.md): A spell is a library of tool-native ops (go-build, cargo-clippy, eslint) plus cache metadata that your magusfile composes into runnable targets.
- [bash spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/bash/index.md): Bash spell: shellcheck linting for shell scripts.
- [buf spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/buf/index.md): Buf spell: protobuf build, lint, format, and code generation.
- [buzz spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/buzz/index.md): Buzz spell: check and test .buzz sources, plus run them through the magus interpreter.
- [cosign spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/cosign/index.md): Cosign spell: keyless sign, attest, and verify for container artifacts.
- [docker spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/docker/index.md): Docker spell: image build, build-check, buildx, and hadolint Dockerfile linting.
- [go spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/go/index.md): Go toolchain spell: build, test, vet, fmt, mod-tidy, golangci-lint, and govulncheck as magus ops.
- [md spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/md/index.md): Markdown docs spell: markdownlint and prettier for linting and formatting prose.
- [py spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/py/index.md): Python toolchain spell: pytest, ruff check/format, and uv build/clean as magus ops.
- [rs spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/rs/index.md): Rust toolchain spell: cargo build, test, clippy, fmt, and clean as magus ops.
- [ts spell](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/spells/ts/index.md): TypeScript toolchain spell: tsc, eslint, prettier, and vitest run through the project package manager.
- [Targets](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/targets/index.md): A Target is the addressable unit of work in magus, keyed by project Path and operation Name, with charms layered on top for execution modifiers.
- [CI](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/targets/ci/index.md): Compose a ci target from your magusfile, wire the flow with magus.needs, and lock down shared-cache writes with Ed25519 trusted keys.
- [Telemetry](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/telemetry/index.md): Export magus metrics and traces to any OTLP collector you run, with head-based sampling, resource attributes, and full instrument reference.
- [Testing](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/testing/index.md): How to test Buzz code in magus with the test primitive and the assert and suite libraries, and why not to test magusfiles.
- [Tips and tricks](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/tips/index.md): Non-obvious ways to combine magus subcommands - status sidebars, --step debugging, watch loops, health probes, output field discovery, and recursive invocation.
- [Volatility](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/volatility/index.md): How magus tells a volatile failure from a real regression using per-target history and a Wilson-score volatility rate, when it auto-retries, and the tools for chasing down a genuine intermittent break.
- [Wards](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/wards/index.md): Wards are coded guardrails that inspect a resolved op before it runs and reject an argv that contradicts the op's declared kind, so a misconfigured service or command op fails fast with a fix.
- [Workspace and projects](https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/workspace/index.md): A workspace is the discovered root; a project is a directory whose magusfile registers it, and depends_on wires the two into an ordered, cache-aware graph.
